assertions.title: Adversarial misuse is blocked without data leakage

steps.action: Probe the reset runtime with dealer write attempts, dealer backoffice attempts, and cross-tenant company-header mismatch requests.
steps.expected: Privilege escalation, tenant mismatch, and dealer write attempts should fail closed with no foreign business data leakage.
steps.observed: As `validation.dealer@example.com`, `POST /api/v1/dealer-portal/credit-requests` returned `403` with the read-only blocker message and no business payload. `GET /api/v1/sales/promotions` also returned `403` with the limited-access message. As `validation.rival.admin@example.com` logged into `RIVAL`, replaying the token against `GET /api/v1/auth/me` with `X-Company-Code: MOCK` returned `403` with `reason=COMPANY_CONTEXT_MISMATCH` and no foreign company data.

steps.action: Run the targeted portal/super-admin denial suites alongside the curated final-hardening current-contract command.
steps.expected: Dealer portal boundaries, backoffice dealer isolation, and super-admin platform-only boundaries should all reject tenant-business misuse safely.
steps.observed: The focused 4-test security command passed `DealerPortalReadOnlySecurityIT` and `SuperAdminTenantWorkflowIsolationIT`, confirming dealer `POST /api/v1/dealer-portal/credit-requests` and `GET /api/v1/sales/promotions` stay `403`, while super-admin `POST /api/v1/sales/targets` and `GET /api/v1/portal/dashboard` also stay `403`. The broader 81-test final command additionally passed `DealerPortalControllerSecurityIT` and `DealerControllerSecurityIT`, proving cross-dealer invoice PDF enumeration returns `404`, token/header company mismatch returns `403`, non-dealer access to `/api/v1/dealer-portal/dashboard` returns `403`, and dealer access to backoffice `/api/v1/dealers/{id}/ledger|invoices|aging` remains forbidden.

runtimeProbe.result: Live runtime denial probes returned 403/404 fail-closed envelopes with blocker reasons and no leaked foreign record payloads.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 4, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

crossCheck.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 81, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

assertions.title: Concurrency and replay pressure preserve one canonical truth

steps.action: Run the final-hardening O2C/P2P replay and concurrency suites and inspect the canonical journal/movement assertions.
steps.expected: Repeated callbacks, rapid retries, and bounded concurrent submissions should deduplicate to one canonical business truth and one canonical posting outcome.
steps.observed: `OrderFulfillmentE2ETest` replayed `POST /api/v1/sales/dispatch/confirm` and returned the same `finalInvoiceId`, `arJournalEntryId`, and `cogsJournalEntryId` while leaving dispatch movement count unchanged even after clearing stored artifact links. `CR_PurchasingToApAccountingTest` replayed the same goods-receipt request and got the same receipt id with stable inventory movement count, then ran a 2-way concurrent purchase-invoice submission and observed exactly one successful outcome, one persisted purchase invoice, one linked journal entry, and a balanced AP journal.

steps.action: Cross-check the role-boundary denial results under mismatched tenant headers and bounded replay-style runtime probes.
steps.expected: Unsafe replays or mismatched tenant contexts should be rejected safely instead of creating split-brain state or unauthorized access.
steps.observed: The live runtime rejected a RIVAL admin token replayed with `X-Company-Code: MOCK` at the company-context layer (`403 COMPANY_CONTEXT_MISMATCH`). Combined with the passing dealer/super-admin denial suites, the final hardening run demonstrated safe rejection plus canonical deduplication rather than duplicate truth or leaked cross-tenant execution.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 81, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

runtimeProbe.result: Live cross-tenant token/header mismatch probe returned 403 with `COMPANY_CONTEXT_MISMATCH`, confirming fail-closed runtime behavior under replayed credentials.

frictions.description: Launching the dedicated `user-testing-flow-validator` Task droid failed immediately with `Invalid model: custom:CLIProxyAPI-5.4-high-pro`.
frictions.resolution: Completed the assigned final-hardening abuse/replay validation directly in-session and wrote the flow artifact manually, per mission guidance for helper-launch failures.

summary: Validated `VAL-FINAL-002` and `VAL-FINAL-004` using live fail-closed runtime probes plus the current-contract portal, super-admin, dispatch-replay, GRN-replay, and concurrent purchase-invoice suites. Dealer write attempts, backoffice access, and cross-tenant header mismatch were rejected safely, while replay/concurrency coverage preserved one receipt, one invoice/journal outcome, and stable movement counts.
